title: Winzo Games Private Limited vs Bajaar Llc And Ors on 10 March, 2023
doc:
Winzo Games Private Limited vs Bajaar Llc And Ors on 10 March, 2023

Author: Sanjeev Narula

Bench: Sanjeev Narula

                          $~17
                          *      IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI AT NEW DELHI
                          +      CS(COMM) 135/2023
                                 WINZO GAMES PRIVATE LIMITED                              ..... Plaintiff
                                                    Through:      Mr. Abhishek Malhotra and Mr.
                                                                  Harsh B. Buch, Advocates

                                                    versus

                                 BAJAAR LLC AND ORS                                      ..... Defendants
                                                    Through:      Ms. Mamta Rani Jha, Mr. Rohan
                                                                  Ahuja, Ms. Shruttima Ehersa, Mr.
                                                                  Vatsalya Vishal and Ms. Amishi
                                                                  Sodani, Advocates for D-6

                                 CORAM:
                                 HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJEEV NARULA
                                                    ORDER

% 10.03.2023 I.A. No. 4735/2023 (for exemption from filing original, certified, types copies of dim annexures etc.)

1. Exemption is granted, subject to all just exceptions.

2. Applicant shall file legible and clearer copies of exempted documents, compliant with practice rules, before the next date of hearing.

3. Disposed of.

I.A. No. 4736/2023 (for filing additional documents)

4. This is an application seeking leave to file additional documents under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

5. Applicant, if it wishes to file additional documents at a later stage, Signature Not Verified Digitally Signed CS(COMM) 135/2023 Page 1 of 8 By:AKANSHA SINGH Signing Date:11.03.2023 20:22:13 shall do so strictly as per the provisions of the said Act.

6. Disposed of.

I.A. No. 4737/2023 (for extension of time for filing court fees)

7. For grounds and reasons stated in the application, the application is allowed. Plaintiff is granted two weeks' time from today to file deficient court fees.

8. Disposed of.

I.A. No. 4738/2023 (for exemption from pre-institution mediation)

9. Having regard to the facts of the present case and in light of the judgement of Division Bench of this Court in Chandra Kishore Chaurasia v. R.A. Perfumery Works Private Ltd.,1 exemption from attempting pre- institution mediation is allowed.

10. Disposed of.

CS(COMM) 135/2023

11. Let the plaint be registered as a suit.

12. Issue summons. Summons are accepted by Ms. Mamta Rani Jha, counsel for Defendant No. 6 [Google LLC]. Upon filing of process fee, issue summons to remaining Defendants by all permissible modes. Summons shall state that written statement shall be filed by Defendants within 30 days from date of receipt of summons. Along with written statement, Defendants shall also file an affidavit of admission/denial of Plaintiff's documents, without which the written statement shall not be taken on record.

Signature Not Verified Digitally Signed CS(COMM) 135/2023 Page 2 of 8 By:AKANSHA SINGH Signing Date:11.03.2023 20:22:13

13. Liberty is given to Plaintiff to file a replication within 15 days of the receipt of the written statements. Along with the replication, if any, filed by the Plaintiff, an affidavit of admission/denial of documents of the Defendants, be filed by the Plaintiff, without which the replication shall not be taken on record. If any of the parties wish to seek inspection of any documents, the same shall be sought and given within the timelines.

14. List before the Joint Registrar for marking of exhibits on 04th May, 2023. It is made clear that any party unjustifiably denying documents would be liable to be burdened with costs.

15. List before Court for framing of issues thereafter on 07th August, 2023.

I.A. No. 4734/2023 (under Order 39 Rule 1 & 2 r/w S. 151 of CPC, 1908)

16. Plaintiffs have filed the present suit seeking inter alia permanent and mandatory injunction restraining the infringement of trademarks, passing off, rendition of accounts, and damages.

17. The case set out in the plaint is as follows:

17.1 Plaintiff, a digital gaming and technology company, operates an online skill based digital gaming platform under its registered trademark and brand name "WinZO"/ "WINZO" [hereinafter "Plaintiff's Platform"]. 17.2 Plaintiff's Platform is the 'biggest social gaming app' in India in terms of number of games, languages, and formats of games available on the platform. It has a user base of more than 100 million registered users with a strong presence in tier-2 cities as well as rural India. 17.3 Plaintiff has various registrations of the marks "WinZO" and "WinZO 1 Neutral citation: 2022/DHC/004454.
Signature Not Verified Digitally Signed CS(COMM) 135/2023 Page 3 of 8 By:AKANSHA SINGH Signing Date:11.03.2023 20:22:13

games", and variants/ formatives thereof [hereinafter "Plaintiff's mark(s)"], which are detailed at paragraph 17 of the plaint. Details of Plaintiff's annual revenues for the period 2019-20 to 2021-22 are also provided at paragraph 19 of the plaint.

17.4 Defendant No. 1 [Bajaar LLC], a corporation registered under the laws of United States of America (USA), is operating an un-registered gaming application under the infringing name "WINZOS!" as also an online gaming platform under the domain name "https://www.winzos.com/"

[hereinafter "Infringing Website"]. Per the terms of service available on said website, it appears that Defendant No. 1 has entered into an agreement with Defendant No. 2 [Learny Inc.], a corporation formed under the laws of the State of Delaware, USA, which operates a website viz. "www.learny.co" that redirects to the Infringing Website. A screenshot of Infringing Website is as under:
Signature Not Verified Digitally Signed CS(COMM) 135/2023 Page 4 of 8 By:AKANSHA SINGH Signing Date:11.03.2023 20:22:13
17.5. Infringing Website of Defendants No. 1 and 2 is registered in the name of Defendant No. 3 [Namecheap Inc.], who is also the domain name registrar of the aforesaid websites. Defendant No. 4 [Surojit Niyogi] stated to be founder of Defendant No. 1 is said to be carrying on business through the company viz. Pad Pro Software Limited in India (whereof Defendant No. 4 is also a director).
17.6 Defendants No. 1, 2 and 4 are alter egos of one another with common intent of aiding the acts of infringement, passing off et al with a view to make illicit commercial gains at the cost of Plaintiff.
Signature Not Verified Digitally Signed CS(COMM) 135/2023 Page 5 of 8 By:AKANSHA SINGH Signing Date:11.03.2023 20:22:13
18. Mr. Abhishek Malhotra, counsel for Plaintiff, states that during routine checks in October, 2022, Plaintiff came across Defendant No. 1's application on the Apple Inc.'s App Store bearing the name "WINZOS!"

[hereinafter "Infringing Application"], as also on the Google Play Store. Subsequently, on further checks, Plaintiff discovered the Infringing Website, which is accessible to public in India and information available thereon confirms that Defendant No.1 provides services of online games and prizes through Infringing Application which are nearly identical to services provided by Plaintiff through its online gaming platform under its trade name "WinZO"/ "WINGO GAMES" / "WINZO". When the cease-and- desist notice was issued, Defendant No. 1 responded by stating that its use is not within India and that it is unaware of Plaintiff's presence.

19. Mr. Malhotra further states Defendants No. 1 and 2, who are operating in the arena of online gaming, have adopted the mark "WINZOS!"

to ride on the goodwill and reputation of Plaintiff, which is widely recognised and well-known by internet users for its services. The term "WINZOS!" is nearly identical to Plaintiff's mark "WINZO". Consumers are bound to be confused which would lead them to Infringing Application and Infringing Website, thus causing wrongful association, loss of revenue and dilution of Plaintiff's marks and brand name.

20. The Court has considered the aforenoted facts and submissions. The gaming services which is being operated by Defendants No. 1, 2 and 4 under the mark "WINZOS!" is prima facie deceptively similar to Plaintiff's marks. In view of the above, Court is satisfied that Plaintiff has made out a prima facie case. Balance of convenience also lies in favour of Plaintiff and against Defendants No. 1, 2 and 4, and irreparable loss would be caused in case they Signature Not Verified Digitally Signed CS(COMM) 135/2023 Page 6 of 8 By:AKANSHA SINGH Signing Date:11.03.2023 20:22:13 continue to offer their services bearing the "WINZOS!" mark which is deceptively similar to that of Plaintiffs. Accordingly, till the next date of hearing, the following ad-interim relief is granted:

20.1 Defendants No. 1, 2 and 4, its principal officers, promoters, employees, associates, affiliates, agents et al. are restrained from using Plaintiff's registered marks viz. Winzo, WinZO Games, WINZO et al. or any other mark or trade indicia which is confusingly similar to Plaintiff's said marks, in any form or manner, whatsoever, including in respect of any product and service for which Plaintiff has obtained registration including inter alia gaming services, so as to infringe Plaintiff's exclusive rights in said trademarks.
20.2 Defendants No. 1, 2 and 4, its principal officers, promoters, employees, associates, affiliates, agents et al. are restrained from using Plaintiff's registered marks viz. Winzo, WinZO Games, WINZO et al. or any other mark or trade indicia which is confusingly similar to Plaintiff's said marks, in any form or manner, whatsoever, including by way of trademark, trading name, trading style, domain name, keywords, metatags, etc. in respect to any product or service including gaming services, for which Plaintiff has been using its said brands and trademarks, so as to pass off its services as those of Plaintiff.
21. Compliance of Order XXXIX Rule 3 of CPC be done within one week from today.
22. Ms. Mamta Rani Jha, counsel for Defendant No. 6 [Google LLC] states that on a specific request from Plaintiff, the Infringing Application was made inaccessible from Google Play Store in India and accordingly, the application is no longer available for download in India. This was also Signature Not Verified Digitally Signed CS(COMM) 135/2023 Page 7 of 8 By:AKANSHA SINGH Signing Date:11.03.2023 20:22:13 intimated to Plaintiff on 18th October, 2022. In view of the foregoing, she states that Defendant No. 6 should be deleted from the array of parties. To this, Mr Malhotra states that the Infringing Application though inaccessible, is still visible on the Google Play Store within India.
23. In light of the above submissions, Ms. Jha is directed to place the said communication/ relevant documents on record, within three weeks from today.
24. List on 07th August, 2023.

SANJEEV NARULA, J MARCH 10, 2023 tp Signature Not Verified Digitally Signed CS(COMM) 135/2023 Page 8 of 8 By:AKANSHA SINGH Signing Date:11.03.2023 20:22:13
